The correct resolution of a dominant seventh chord typically resolves the chordal seventh down by step and the leading tone up to the tonic. In option C, the dominant seventh in F major (G–B–C–E) resolves to the tonic chord (F–A–C–D), with the seventh (E) moving down to D and the leading tone (B) moving up to C, which follows standard voice-leading rules.
